page: UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-128GB is a compute-oriented server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration allocates 64 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio on a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 500 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier CPU capabilities. The server achieves top 10% performance in stress-ng single-core and multi-core evaluations, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 89600.48. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also ranks in the top 10% at 574.99 GB/sec. Memory performance is strong, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 8284.04 GB/sec and large-block read/write bandwidths placing in the top 25%. However, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, and LLM inference speed for prompt processing on a small model is poor, registering at 69.99 tokens/sec.

From a resource-density perspective, this instance offers high compute density with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The shared CPU allocation model represents a potential tradeoff in environments requiring strict, sustained single-tenant performance consistency. Given its strong database operations benchmark and high multi-core throughput, this server is highly suited for multi-threaded data processing, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized computational workloads.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-128GB is a compute-focused virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with an x86_64 architecture, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 500 GB local SSD. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 158588.7 and a PassMark CPU score of 89600.48, both placing in the top 10% of benchmarks. While single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels and LLM inference speeds are low, the server excels in memory bandwidth and database operations. This hardware profile is highly suited for parallelized computational tasks, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded database workloads.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-128GB is a KVM-based server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU performance for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-64xCPU-128GB is a KVM server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and bundled SSD storage for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with x86_64 architecture
128 GB of system memory and a bundled 500 GB local SSD
Top-tier multi-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance
Strong memory bandwidth and database operations throughput
Best suited for parallel data processing and high-throughput web serving
